title: Software Engineer Intern — Folio3 Pakistan
content:

- **Summary:** Worked as a software engineer intern in Folio3 where I contributed to scalable backend services and AI-driven solutions, focusing on performance, maintainability, and industry-level deployment practices.
- **Details:** As a Software Engineer Intern at Folio3 Pakistan, I contributed to the development of SnapVault, a cutting-edge mobile application designed for private and seamless image distribution. My primary focus was on backend development, where I leveraged FastAPI to build secure and efficient APIs, manage data, and implement advanced logic, including computer vision techniques crucial for ensuring user privacy and optimizing image handling. I also worked on the frontend using React Native, enhancing the user experience and ensuring a smooth and intuitive interface for secure image sharing. Additionally, I gained hands-on experience with deployment on AWS EC2, ensuring scalability and reliability of the application.

title: BB Lounge - Premium Café Website
content:
- **Short description:** A modern, responsive business website for BB Lounge in Dammam, Saudi Arabia, showcasing premium shisha flavors, café menu, event bookings, customer testimonials, gallery, and online reservation options with WhatsApp integration.
- **Tech stack:** React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3, Responsive Design, WhatsApp API, Google Maps Embed
- **Details:** BB Lounge is a premium café and shisha lounge website designed to provide customers with a seamless online experience while highlighting the lounge's modern atmosphere and services. The website features an elegant, mobile-friendly interface with dedicated sections for the gallery, complete food and beverage menu, premium shisha flavors, exclusive deals, customer testimonials, event bookings, business hours, location, and contact information.

The platform allows visitors to explore the lounge's offerings, reserve tables, and place orders directly through WhatsApp for a quick and convenient booking experience. Social media integration, Google Maps navigation, and an Instagram showcase help strengthen the brand's online presence while providing visitors with easy access to updates and directions.
- **Links:** live: https://bb-lounge.vercel.app/

title: CrickVision (Featured)
content:
- **Short description:** An AI-powered mobile cricket analysis platform that detects the cricket ball, tracks its trajectory, and estimates delivery speed using computer vision, making professional-grade analytics accessible to any player with a smartphone.
- **Tech stack:** React Native, Expo, JavaScript, FastAPI, Python, YOLOv10n, OpenCV, PyTorch, Supabase, PostgreSQL, Uvicorn, Axios, react-native-svg, expo-camera, expo-av, Google Colab
- **Details:** CrickVision is a final year design project built to bridge the gap between expensive professional cricket analysis systems like Hawk-Eye and the everyday player. Using a custom-trained YOLOv10n object detection model, the system processes cricket delivery videos to detect the ball frame-by-frame, generate visual trajectory overlays, and calculate real-world ball speed.
Players and coaches can record live deliveries or upload pre-recorded videos directly through the React Native mobile app. The backend, built with FastAPI and served via Uvicorn, handles all AI inference and video processing asynchronously for efficient performance. Results are visualized on-device using SVG overlays, and all analysis history is stored per user through Supabase.
The project was developed over 8 Agile Scrum sprints from October 2025 to May 2026, covering dataset collection (~40000 labeled frames), model training on Google Colab, backend development, frontend integration, and testing. CrickVision targets cricket academies, coaching centers, and individual players across South Asia as an affordable alternative to broadcast-grade analytics systems.
- **Links:** live: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/18cDmDdQTLaTZuSuNc1PUvxhXQTITIaZE?usp=sharing | backendRepo: https://github.com/UsmanChaudhary115/CrickVision-Backend.git | frontendRepo: https://github.com/Rao-Mubashir/CrickNet_Fyp.git

title: SnapVault - AI-Powered Photo Distribution System (Featured)
content:

- **Tech stack:** React Native, FastAPI, Python, Computer Vision, AWS EC2
- **Details:** Developed a cutting-edge mobile application for private and seamless image distribution using React Native and FastAPI. The system leverages facial recognition to automatically tag and group photos, enabling secure private sharing within groups for seamless collaboration. Backend developed with FastAPI for secure and efficient APIs, data management, and advanced logic including computer vision techniques crucial for ensuring user privacy and optimizing image handling.
